channel.title: Posts on Entre Dos Tierras
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Entre Dos Tierras

item.title: Asado de Boda: 120 Years of History on One Plate
item.description: What is Asado de Boda? Asado de Boda is slow-braised pork in a rich, sweet-and-spicy red chile sauce — one of the handful of dishes that put Zacatecas on Mexico’s culinary map. You’ll find it at every big celebration: weddings, quinceañeras, baptisms. Its preparation is considered a symbol of abundance. In the north-central region of Mexico, there is simply no wedding without it. It’s not just food. It’s a tradition.
